disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Severe, rapidly worsening breathlessness and air-hunger developing over the 12–36 hours after a witnessed choking/aspiration event
trigger_question_examples: When did the breathing trouble start?
Did anything happen just before it began?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He choked badly on some food about a day and a half ago, and since then his breathing has got worse and worse — the last 12 hours have been really bad.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Very low home oximeter oxygen saturation (84%) that is not improving with rest in a well-ventilated room
trigger_question_examples: Do you have an oxygen monitor at home? What does it read?
Is the oxygen number going up or down?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: The little finger monitor says 84 and it's not going up no matter what we do — he's just resting in a well-aired room.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No relief from sitting upright; profound air-hunger and exhaustion from the work of breathing
trigger_question_examples: Does sitting up or any position help him breathe?
How hard is he having to work to breathe?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Sitting him up doesn't help at all. He's gasping for air and worn out from trying to breathe.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Dusky/blue lips and new drowsiness or confusion — a sign of severe oxygen lack
trigger_question_examples: What colour are his lips or face?
Is he more drowsy, confused or hard to rouse than normal?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: His lips have gone a bit blue and he's gone quite drowsy and muddled — not really himself.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No prior history of heart failure or being woken breathless at night; not on any cardiac medication
trigger_question_examples: Has he ever been told he has heart failure or a weak heart?
Does he normally wake at night short of breath, or take fluid/water tablets?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, he's never had heart trouble and doesn't take any heart or water tablets. This is completely new.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Feels hot/feverish and recently finished a course of antibiotics after the choking episode
trigger_question_examples: Has he had a fever or felt hot?
Is he on or has he recently taken any medicines?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He feels hot — the thermometer said 38.1 — and he just finished antibiotic tablets they gave him after the choking.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Dry, ineffective cough without pink frothy sputum
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He's got a dry cough that doesn't bring anything up — no froth or pink stuff.
